127.reason: Error: Should be Visited[delV]=1; using i instead of delV causes the wrong vertex to be marked as visited, leading to incorrect BFS traversal after deletion.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Should be Visited[delV]=1; using i instead of delV causes the wrong vertex to be marked as visited, leading to incorrect BFS traversal after deletion.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Should be Visited[delV]=1; using i instead of delV causes the wrong vertex to be marked as visited, leading to incorrect BFS traversal after deletion.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Should be Visited[delV]=1; This causes the deleted vertex to not be marked visited, so BFS traverses it.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: 'i' is the loop variable left at 'n' after the preceding for loop, not the vertex to delete. Should be 'Visited[delV]=1;'. This causes the deleted vertex not to be marked visited, so BFS still visits it.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The variable 'i' is used here, but after the previous loop, 'i' equals 'n'. It should be 'delV' to mark the deleted vertex as visited.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The variable 'i' is used here, but after the previous loop, 'i' equals 'n'. It should be 'delV' to mark the deleted vertex as visited.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The variable 'i' is equal to 'n' after the loop, so Visited[i] accesses out-of-bounds memory. It should be Visited[delV]=1 instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This line incorrectly sets Visited[i] (which is Visited[n]) instead of Visited[delV]. After the loop, i equals n, so Visited[n] is out of bounds and the deletion vertex is not marked as visited.]
